Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other names = | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = October 9 | Rituals = }} [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tomobayashi Uji Shrine''' (伴林氏神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, in the Hayashi area of the former Haishi-go, Shiki District, Kawachi Province. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} and previously held the rank of {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}}. It was once referred to as the "Western {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|12=draft}}." == Deities == * Primary deity - {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft}} (たかむすひのかみ) * Secondary deities - {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと), {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天押日命|12=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) The shrine's name suggests that it was originally established by the {{ill|Tomobayashi clan|ja|伴林氏|12=draft}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}}, to worship their ancestral deities. Takami-Musubi no Kami is identified as the fifth-generation ancestor of Ame-no-Oshihi, who in turn is the third-generation ancestor of Michinoomi no Mikoto. == History == [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, chouzusha.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Chōzuya}}]] The founding year of the shrine is unknown. According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}, an entry dated the 26th day of the 2nd month in the 9th year of Jōgan (867) mentions that the Hayashi Uji Shrine of Kawachi Province was recognized as a state shrine. Another entry from the 20th day of the 12th month in the 15th year of Jōgan (873) records the elevation of the enshrined deity Ame-no-Oshihi to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was burned down by the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}. After the extinction of the Tomobayashi clan, local residents reconstructed the shrine as a place to worship their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the shrine's identification as Tomobayashi Uji Shrine was debated among {{ill|Kokugaku|lt=Kokugaku scholars|ja|国学|12=draft}}. Despite inquiries by Edo officials, local village leaders at the time claimed it was a shrine dedicated to Gozu Tennō. By the late Edo period, the shrine became recognized as a site for worshiping the ancestral deity of the Ōtomo clan, influenced by the work of {{ill|Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|12=draft}}, a Kokugaku scholar from the village.Keita Endō, "Studies and Restoration of Shikinaisha: Investigation of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine" (First published in ''Shidai Nihonshi'', Vol. 5, 2002 / Collected in Endō, ''Formation of the Nihon Shoki and Related Materials'', Hanawa Shobō, 2015). In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. In 1932 (Shōwa 7), a survey commemorating the 50th anniversary of the {{ill|Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|ja|軍人勅諭|12=draft}} confirmed that this shrine is the only one in Japan dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto, the military deity of the Ōtomo clan. Consequently, it gained attention as the "Western Yasukuni Shrine." This designation heavily relied on the interpretation of Kokugaku scholar and historian {{ill|Ikuta namekine|ja|生田目経徳|12=draft}}. Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=draft}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|12=draft}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|12=draft}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tomobayashi Shrine}}

Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=draft}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|12=draft}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|12=draft}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tomobayashi Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tomobayashi Uji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}} Hayashi 3-chome 6-30 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 34 | Latitude seconds = 32.649 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 36 | Longitude seconds = 40.439 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Takami-Musubi no Kami|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other names = | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = October 9 | Rituals = }} [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tomobayashi Uji Shrine''' (伴林氏神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, in the Hayashi area of the former Haishi-go, Shiki District, Kawachi Province. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} and previously held the rank of {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}}. It was once referred to as the "Western {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|12=draft}}." == Deities == * Primary deity - {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft}} (たかむすひのかみ) * Secondary deities - {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと), {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天押日命|12=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) The shrine's name suggests that it was originally established by the {{ill|Tomobayashi clan|ja|伴林氏|12=draft}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}}, to worship their ancestral deities. Takami-Musubi no Kami is identified as the fifth-generation ancestor of Ame-no-Oshihi, who in turn is the third-generation ancestor of Michinoomi no Mikoto. == History == [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, chouzusha.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Chōzuya}}]] The founding year of the shrine is unknown. According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}, an entry dated the 26th day of the 2nd month in the 9th year of Jōgan (867) mentions that the Hayashi Uji Shrine of Kawachi Province was recognized as a state shrine. Another entry from the 20th day of the 12th month in the 15th year of Jōgan (873) records the elevation of the enshrined deity Ame-no-Oshihi to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was burned down by the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}. After the extinction of the Tomobayashi clan, local residents reconstructed the shrine as a place to worship their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the shrine's identification as Tomobayashi Uji Shrine was debated among {{ill|Kokugaku|lt=Kokugaku scholars|ja|国学|12=draft}}. Despite inquiries by Edo officials, local village leaders at the time claimed it was a shrine dedicated to Gozu Tennō. By the late Edo period, the shrine became recognized as a site for worshiping the ancestral deity of the Ōtomo clan, influenced by the work of {{ill|Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|12=draft}}, a Kokugaku scholar from the village.Keita Endō, "Studies and Restoration of Shikinaisha: Investigation of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine" (First published in ''Shidai Nihonshi'', Vol. 5, 2002 / Collected in Endō, ''Formation of the Nihon Shoki and Related Materials'', Hanawa Shobō, 2015). In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. In 1932 (Shōwa 7), a survey commemorating the 50th anniversary of the {{ill|Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|ja|軍人勅諭|12=draft}} confirmed that this shrine is the only one in Japan dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto, the military deity of the Ōtomo clan. Consequently, it gained attention as the "Western Yasukuni Shrine." This designation heavily relied on the interpretation of Kokugaku scholar and historian {{ill|Ikuta namekine|ja|生田目経徳|12=draft}}. Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=draft}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|12=draft}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|12=draft}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tomobayashi Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tomobayashi Uji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}} Hayashi 3-chome 6-30 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 34 | Latitude seconds = 32.649 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 36 | Longitude seconds = 40.439 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Takami-Musubi no Kami|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other names = | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = October 9 | Rituals = }} [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tomobayashi Uji Shrine''' (伴林氏神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the Hayashi area of the former Haishi-go, Shiki District, Kawachi Province. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and previously held the rank of {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was once referred to as the "Western {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}." == Deities == * Primary deity - {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}} (たかむすひのかみ) * Secondary deities - {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft|12=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと), {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天押日命|12=draft|12=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) The shrine's name suggests that it was originally established by the {{ill|Tomobayashi clan|ja|伴林氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, to worship their ancestral deities. Takami-Musubi no Kami is identified as the fifth-generation ancestor of Ame-no-Oshihi, who in turn is the third-generation ancestor of Michinoomi no Mikoto. == History == [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, chouzusha.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Chōzuya}}]] The founding year of the shrine is unknown. According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, an entry dated the 26th day of the 2nd month in the 9th year of Jōgan (867) mentions that the Hayashi Uji Shrine of Kawachi Province was recognized as a state shrine. Another entry from the 20th day of the 12th month in the 15th year of Jōgan (873) records the elevation of the enshrined deity Ame-no-Oshihi to the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was burned down by the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft|12=draft}}. After the extinction of the Tomobayashi clan, local residents reconstructed the shrine as a place to worship their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine's identification as Tomobayashi Uji Shrine was debated among {{ill|Kokugaku|lt=Kokugaku scholars|ja|国学|12=draft|12=draft}}. Despite inquiries by Edo officials, local village leaders at the time claimed it was a shrine dedicated to Gozu Tennō. By the late Edo period, the shrine became recognized as a site for worshiping the ancestral deity of the Ōtomo clan, influenced by the work of {{ill|Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Kokugaku scholar from the village.Keita Endō, "Studies and Restoration of Shikinaisha: Investigation of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine" (First published in ''Shidai Nihonshi'', Vol. 5, 2002 / Collected in Endō, ''Formation of the Nihon Shoki and Related Materials'', Hanawa Shobō, 2015). In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. In 1932 (Shōwa 7), a survey commemorating the 50th anniversary of the {{ill|Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|ja|軍人勅諭|12=draft|12=draft}} confirmed that this shrine is the only one in Japan dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto, the military deity of the Ōtomo clan. Consequently, it gained attention as the "Western Yasukuni Shrine." This designation heavily relied on the interpretation of Kokugaku scholar and historian {{ill|Ikuta namekine|ja|生田目経徳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the Completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=draft|12=draft}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Text replacement - "completion tier" to "Completion tier]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tomobayashi Shrine}}

According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an entry dated the 26th day of the 2nd month in the 9th year of Jōgan (867) mentions that the Hayashi Uji Shrine of Kawachi Province was recognized as a state shrine. Another entry from the 20th day of the 12th month in the 15th year of Jōgan (873) records the elevation of the enshrined deity Ame-no-Oshihi to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was burned down by the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the extinction of the Tomobayashi clan, local residents reconstructed the shrine as a place to worship their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine's identification as Tomobayashi Uji Shrine was debated among {{ill|Kokugaku|lt=Kokugaku scholars|ja|国学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Despite inquiries by Edo officials, local village leaders at the time claimed it was a shrine dedicated to Gozu Tennō. By the late Edo period, the shrine became recognized as a site for worshiping the ancestral deity of the Ōtomo clan, influenced by the work of {{ill|Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Kokugaku scholar from the village.Keita Endō, "Studies and Restoration of Shikinaisha: Investigation of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine" (First published in ''Shidai Nihonshi'', Vol. 5, 2002 / Collected in Endō, ''Formation of the Nihon Shoki and Related Materials'', Hanawa Shobō, 2015). In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. In 1932 (Shōwa 7), a survey commemorating the 50th anniversary of the {{ill|Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|ja|軍人勅諭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} confirmed that this shrine is the only one in Japan dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto, the military deity of the Ōtomo clan. Consequently, it gained attention as the "Western Yasukuni Shrine." This designation heavily relied on the interpretation of Kokugaku scholar and historian {{ill|Ikuta namekine|ja|生田目経徳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the Completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Text replacement - "completion tier" to "Completion tier]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tomobayashi Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tomobayashi Uji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hayashi 3-chome 6-30 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 34 | Latitude seconds = 32.649 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 36 | Longitude seconds = 40.439 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Takami-Musubi no Kami|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other names = | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = October 9 | Rituals = }} [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tomobayashi Uji Shrine''' (伴林氏神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the Hayashi area of the former Haishi-go, Shiki District, Kawachi Province. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and previously held the Rank of {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was once referred to as the "Western {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." == Deities == * Primary deity - {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (たかむすひのかみ) * Secondary deities - {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと), {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天押日命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) The shrine's name suggests that it was originally established by the {{ill|Tomobayashi clan|ja|伴林氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to worship their ancestral deities. Takami-Musubi no Kami is identified as the fifth-generation ancestor of Ame-no-Oshihi, who in turn is the third-generation ancestor of Michinoomi no Mikoto. == History == [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, chouzusha.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Chōzuya}}]] The founding year of the shrine is unknown. According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an entry dated the 26th day of the 2nd month in the 9th year of Jōgan (867) mentions that the Hayashi Uji Shrine of Kawachi Province was recognized as a state shrine. Another entry from the 20th day of the 12th month in the 15th year of Jōgan (873) records the elevation of the enshrined deity Ame-no-Oshihi to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was burned down by the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the extinction of the Tomobayashi clan, local residents reconstructed the shrine as a place to worship their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine's identification as Tomobayashi Uji Shrine was debated among {{ill|Kokugaku|lt=Kokugaku scholars|ja|国学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Despite inquiries by Edo officials, local village leaders at the time claimed it was a shrine dedicated to Gozu Tennō. By the late Edo period, the shrine became recognized as a site for worshiping the ancestral deity of the Ōtomo clan, influenced by the work of {{ill|Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Kokugaku scholar from the village.Keita Endō, "Studies and Restoration of Shikinaisha: Investigation of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine" (First published in ''Shidai Nihonshi'', Vol. 5, 2002 / Collected in Endō, ''Formation of the Nihon Shoki and Related Materials'', Hanawa Shobō, 2015). In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. In 1932 (Shōwa 7), a survey commemorating the 50th anniversary of the {{ill|Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|ja|軍人勅諭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} confirmed that this shrine is the only one in Japan dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto, the military deity of the Ōtomo clan. Consequently, it gained attention as the "Western Yasukuni Shrine." This designation heavily relied on the interpretation of Kokugaku scholar and historian {{ill|Ikuta namekine|ja|生田目経徳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the Completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tomobayashi Shrine}}

revision.comment: 27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:伴林氏神社 up to rev 94918840
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 伴林氏神社 |画像 = [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]林3丁目6-30 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 32.649 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 40.439 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神 = [[高皇産霊神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[府社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = [[10月9日]] |神事= }} [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''伴林氏神社'''（ともばやしのうじのじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]林（[[河内国]][[志紀郡]]拝志郷）にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[府社]]。かつては「西の[[靖国神社]]」とも呼ばれた。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[高皇産霊神]]（たかむすひのかみ） * 配祀神 - [[道臣命]]（みちのおみのみこと）、[[天押日命]]（あめのおしひのみこと） 社名から、[[大伴氏]]の支族である[[伴林氏]]が祖神を祀ったものとみられる。高皇産霊神の5代目の子孫が天押日命であり、天押日命の3代目の子孫が道臣命である。 == 歴史 == [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, chouzusha.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|手水舎}}]] 創建年は不詳である。[[日本三代実録]]によると[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）2月26日条に[[河内国]][[志紀郡]]林氏神が官社へ列したとあり、貞観15年（[[873年]]）12月20日には祭神の[[天押日命]]が従五位下に叙せられたという記述がある。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には[[織田信長]]の軍勢によって焼かれてしまい社殿が焼失した。その後、[[伴林氏]]が途絶えると地元の人々によって[[産土神]]を祀る社として再建された。 [[江戸時代]]には伴林氏神社の比定が[[国学者]]によって行われているが、江戸時代の国学者や役人の問い合わせに対しても当時の林村の村役人は[[牛頭天王]]の社であるとして否定的な回答を返している。その林村でも幕末には伴林氏神社を[[大伴氏]]の祖神を祀る社であるとする認識が広まるが、それにはこの村出身の国学者である[[伴林光平]]の影響があったと考えられている遠藤慶太「式内社の考証と顕彰-伴林氏神社の調査から-」（初出:『市大日本史』5号（2002年）/所収:遠藤『日本書紀の形成と諸資料』（塙書房、2015年））。 [[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に村社に列格している。 [[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[軍人勅諭]]下賜50年を記念した調査が行われると、当社が軍事を司どった大伴氏の祖神である[[道臣命]]を祀る日本唯一の神社であることが判明し、「西の[[靖国神社]]」と称されて注目されるようになった。ただし、この決定は当時の[[陸軍省|陸軍次官]]である[[柳川平助]]から調査を委託された国学者・歴史学者の[[生田目経徳]]の持説に基づく要素が強いとされる{{refnest|group=注釈|生田目は他に道臣命を祀っている可能性が指摘されていた[[住吉大伴神社]]や[[大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|大伴神社]]については詳しい調査を行うことなく否認している。}}。これ以降、境内の整備・拡充が行われ、[[1940年]]（昭和15年）に新社殿が完成して遷座が行われた。この時、[[東京府]]（現・[[東京都]]）の靖国神社から1872年（明治5年）に作られた手水舎が寄贈されている。[[1942年]]（昭和17年）2月10日、[[府社]]に昇格した。 社標は[[近衛文麿]]の筆である。 == 境内 == * 本殿（国[[登録有形文化財]]） - [[1940年]]（昭和15年）再建。 * 幣殿（国登録有形文化財） - 1940年（昭和15年）再建。 * 拝殿（国登録有形文化財） - 1940年（昭和15年）再建。 * 透塀（国登録有形文化財） - 1940年（昭和15年）建立。 * 棟門（国登録有形文化財） - 1940年（昭和15年）建立。 * 社務所 * 手水舎（国登録有形文化財） - 1940年（昭和15年）に[[東京府]]（現・[[東京都]]）の[[靖国神社]]から寄進され、現在地に移築。[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に作られたもの。 === 摂・末社 === * 若宮八幡宮（国登録有形文化財） - もとは澤田尋常小学校（現・[[藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|道明寺小学校]]）の奉安殿。 == 文化財 == === 国登録有形文化財 === * 伴林氏神社本殿（建造物） - [[2022年]]（[[令和]]4年）6月29日登録令和4年6月29日文部科学省告示第100号。。 * 伴林氏神社拝殿及び幣殿（建造物） - 2022年（令和4年）6月29日登録。 * 伴林氏神社棟門及び透塀（建造物） - 2022年（令和4年）6月29日登録。 * 伴林氏神社手水舎（建造物） - 2022年（令和4年）6月29日登録。 * 伴林氏神社若宮八幡宮社殿（旧澤田尋常小学校奉安殿）（建造物） - 2022年（令和4年）6月29日登録。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 * 大阪府藤井寺市林3丁目6-30 ;交通アクセス * 鉄道：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[土師ノ里駅]]下車、徒歩約10分（北西へ約800m） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html 伴林氏神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:ともはやしのうしのしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:藤井寺市の建築物]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]]

拝殿 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]林3丁目6-30 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 32.649 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 40.439 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神 = [[高皇産霊神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[府社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = [[10月9日]] |神事= }} [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''伴林氏神社'''（ともばやしのうじのじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]林（[[河内国]][[志紀郡]]拝志郷）にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[府社]]。かつては「西の[[靖国神社]]」とも呼ばれた。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[高皇産霊神]]（たかむすひのかみ） * 配祀神 - [[道臣命]]（みちのおみのみこと）、[[天押日命]]（あめのおしひのみこと） 社名から、[[大伴氏]]の支族である[[伴林氏]]が祖神を祀ったものとみられる。高皇産霊神の5代目の子孫が天押日命であり、天押日命の3代目の子孫が道臣命である。 == 歴史 == [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, chouzusha.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|手水舎}}]] 創建年は不詳である。[[日本三代実録]]によると[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）2月26日条に[[河内国]][[志紀郡]]林氏神が官社へ列したとあり、貞観15年（[[873年]]）12月20日には祭神の[[天押日命]]が従五位下に叙せられたという記述がある。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には[[織田信長]]の軍勢によって焼かれてしまい社殿が焼失した。その後、[[伴林氏]]が途絶えると地元の人々によって[[産土神]]を祀る社として再建された。 [[江戸時代]]には伴林氏神社の比定が[[国学者]]によって行われているが、江戸時代の国学者や役人の問い合わせに対しても当時の林村の村役人は[[牛頭天王]]の社であるとして否定的な回答を返している。その林村でも幕末には伴林氏神社を[[大伴氏]]の祖神を祀る社であるとする認識が広まるが、それにはこの村出身の国学者である[[伴林光平]]の影響があったと考えられている遠藤慶太「式内社の考証と顕彰-伴林氏神社の調査から-」（初出:『市大日本史』5号（2002年）/所収:遠藤『日本書紀の形成と諸資料』（塙書房、2015年））。 [[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に村社に列格している。 [[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）に[[軍人勅諭]]下賜50年を記念した調査が行われると、当社が軍事を司どった大伴氏の祖神である[[道臣命]]を祀る日本唯一の神社であることが判明し、「西の[[靖国神社]]」と称されて注目されるようになった。ただし、この決定は当時の[[陸軍省|陸軍次官]]である[[柳川平助]]から調査を委託された国学者・歴史学者の[[生田目経徳]]の持説に基づく要素が強いとされる{{refnest|group=注釈|生田目は他に道臣命を祀っている可能性が指摘されていた[[住吉大伴神社]]や[[大伴神社 (佐久市望月)|大伴神社]]については詳しい調査を行うことなく否認している。}}。これ以降、境内の整備・拡充が行われ、[[1940年]]（昭和15年）に新社殿が完成して遷座が行われた。この時、[[東京府]]（現・[[東京都]]）の靖国神社から1872年（明治5年）に作られた手水舎が寄贈されている。[[1942年]]（昭和17年）2月10日、[[府社]]に昇格した。 社標は[[近衛文麿]]の筆である。 == 境内 == * 本殿（国[[登録有形文化財]]） - [[1940年]]（昭和15年）再建。 * 幣殿（国登録有形文化財） - 1940年（昭和15年）再建。 * 拝殿（国登録有形文化財） - 1940年（昭和15年）再建。 * 透塀（国登録有形文化財） - 1940年（昭和15年）建立。 * 棟門（国登録有形文化財） - 1940年（昭和15年）建立。 * 社務所 * 手水舎（国登録有形文化財） - 1940年（昭和15年）に[[東京府]]（現・[[東京都]]）の[[靖国神社]]から寄進され、現在地に移築。[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に作られたもの。 === 摂・末社 === * 若宮八幡宮（国登録有形文化財） - もとは澤田尋常小学校（現・[[藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|道明寺小学校]]）の奉安殿。 == 文化財 == === 国登録有形文化財 === * 伴林氏神社本殿（建造物） - [[2022年]]（[[令和]]4年）6月29日登録令和4年6月29日文部科学省告示第100号。。 * 伴林氏神社拝殿及び幣殿（建造物） - 2022年（令和4年）6月29日登録。 * 伴林氏神社棟門及び透塀（建造物） - 2022年（令和4年）6月29日登録。 * 伴林氏神社手水舎（建造物） - 2022年（令和4年）6月29日登録。 * 伴林氏神社若宮八幡宮社殿（旧澤田尋常小学校奉安殿）（建造物） - 2022年（令和4年）6月29日登録。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 * 大阪府藤井寺市林3丁目6-30 ;交通アクセス * 鉄道：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[土師ノ里駅]]下車、徒歩約10分（北西へ約800m） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html 伴林氏神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:ともはやしのうしのしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:藤井寺市の建築物]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tomobayashi Uji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hayashi 3-chome 6-30 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 34 | Latitude seconds = 32.649 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 36 | Longitude seconds = 40.439 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Takami-Musubi no Kami|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other names = | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = October 9 | Rituals = }} [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tomobayashi Uji Shrine''' (伴林氏神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the Hayashi area of the former Haishi-go, Shiki District, Kawachi Province. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and previously held the Rank of {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was once referred to as the "Western {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." == Deities == * Primary deity - {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (たかむすひのかみ) * Secondary deities - {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと), {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天押日命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) The shrine's name suggests that it was originally established by the {{ill|Tomobayashi clan|ja|伴林氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to worship their ancestral deities. Takami-Musubi no Kami is identified as the fifth-generation ancestor of Ame-no-Oshihi, who in turn is the third-generation ancestor of Michinoomi no Mikoto. == History == [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, chouzusha.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Chōzuya}}]] The founding year of the shrine is unknown. According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an entry dated the 26th day of the 2nd month in the 9th year of Jōgan (867) mentions that the Hayashi Uji Shrine of Kawachi Province was recognized as a state shrine. Another entry from the 20th day of the 12th month in the 15th year of Jōgan (873) records the elevation of the enshrined deity Ame-no-Oshihi to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was burned down by the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the extinction of the Tomobayashi clan, local residents reconstructed the shrine as a place to worship their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine's identification as Tomobayashi Uji Shrine was debated among {{ill|Kokugaku|lt=Kokugaku scholars|ja|国学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Despite inquiries by Edo officials, local village leaders at the time claimed it was a shrine dedicated to Gozu Tennō. By the late Edo period, the shrine became recognized as a site for worshiping the ancestral deity of the Ōtomo clan, influenced by the work of {{ill|Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Kokugaku scholar from the village.Keita Endō, "Studies and Restoration of Shikinaisha: Investigation of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine" (First published in ''Shidai Nihonshi'', Vol. 5, 2002 / Collected in Endō, ''Formation of the Nihon Shoki and Related Materials'', Hanawa Shobō, 2015). In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. In 1932 (Shōwa 7), a survey commemorating the 50th anniversary of the {{ill|Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|ja|軍人勅諭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} confirmed that this shrine is the only one in Japan dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto, the military deity of the Ōtomo clan. Consequently, it gained attention as the "Western Yasukuni Shrine." This designation heavily relied on the interpretation of Kokugaku scholar and historian {{ill|Ikuta namekine|ja|生田目経徳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the Completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tomobayashi Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|伴林氏神社|version=94918840|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other names = | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = October 9 | Rituals = }} [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tomobayashi Uji Shrine''' (伴林氏神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the Hayashi area of the former Haishi-go, Shiki District, Kawachi Province. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and previously held the Rank of {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was once referred to as the "Western {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." == Deities == * Primary deity - {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (たかむすひのかみ) * Secondary deities - {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと), {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天押日命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) The shrine's name suggests that it was originally established by the {{ill|Tomobayashi clan|ja|伴林氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to worship their ancestral deities. Takami-Musubi no Kami is identified as the fifth-generation ancestor of Ame-no-Oshihi, who in turn is the third-generation ancestor of Michinoomi no Mikoto. == History == [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, chouzusha.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Chōzuya}}]] The founding year of the shrine is unknown. According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an entry dated the 26th day of the 2nd month in the 9th year of Jōgan (867) mentions that the Hayashi Uji Shrine of Kawachi Province was recognized as a state shrine. Another entry from the 20th day of the 12th month in the 15th year of Jōgan (873) records the elevation of the enshrined deity Ame-no-Oshihi to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was burned down by the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the extinction of the Tomobayashi clan, local residents reconstructed the shrine as a place to worship their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine's identification as Tomobayashi Uji Shrine was debated among {{ill|Kokugaku|lt=Kokugaku scholars|ja|国学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Despite inquiries by Edo officials, local village leaders at the time claimed it was a shrine dedicated to Gozu Tennō. By the late Edo period, the shrine became recognized as a site for worshiping the ancestral deity of the Ōtomo clan, influenced by the work of {{ill|Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Kokugaku scholar from the village.Keita Endō, "Studies and Restoration of Shikinaisha: Investigation of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine" (First published in ''Shidai Nihonshi'', Vol. 5, 2002 / Collected in Endō, ''Formation of the Nihon Shoki and Related Materials'', Hanawa Shobō, 2015). In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. In 1932 (Shōwa 7), a survey commemorating the 50th anniversary of the {{ill|Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|ja|軍人勅諭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} confirmed that this shrine is the only one in Japan dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto, the military deity of the Ōtomo clan. Consequently, it gained attention as the "Western Yasukuni Shrine." This designation heavily relied on the interpretation of Kokugaku scholar and historian {{ill|Ikuta namekine|ja|生田目経徳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the Completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tomobayashi Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|伴林氏神社|version=94918840|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]]

In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine's identification as Tomobayashi Uji Shrine was debated among {{ill|Kokugaku|lt=Kokugaku scholars|ja|国学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Despite inquiries by Edo officials, local village leaders at the time claimed it was a shrine dedicated to Gozu Tennō. By the late Edo period, the shrine became recognized as a site for worshiping the ancestral deity of the Ōtomo clan, influenced by the work of {{ill|Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Kokugaku scholar from the village.Keita Endō, "Studies and Restoration of Shikinaisha: Investigation of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine" (First published in ''Shidai Nihonshi'', Vol. 5, 2002 / Collected in Endō, ''Formation of the Nihon Shoki and Related Materials'', Hanawa Shobō, 2015). In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. In 1932 (Shōwa 7), a survey commemorating the 50th anniversary of the {{ill|Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|ja|軍人勅諭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} confirmed that this shrine is the only one in Japan dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto, the military deity of the Ōtomo clan. Consequently, it gained attention as the "Western Yasukuni Shrine." This designation heavily relied on the interpretation of Kokugaku scholar and historian {{ill|Ikuta namekine|ja|生田目経徳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the Completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{translated page|ja|伴林氏神社|version=94918840|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tomobayashi Uji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hayashi 3-chome 6-30 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 34 | Latitude seconds = 32.649 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 36 | Longitude seconds = 40.439 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Takami-Musubi no Kami|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other names = | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = October 9 | Rituals = }} [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tomobayashi Uji Shrine''' (伴林氏神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the Hayashi area of the former Haishi-go, Shiki District, Kawachi Province. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and previously held the Rank of {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was once referred to as the "Western {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." == Deities == * Primary deity - {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (たかむすひのかみ) * Secondary deities - {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと), {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天押日命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) The shrine's name suggests that it was originally established by the {{ill|Tomobayashi clan|ja|伴林氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to worship their ancestral deities. Takami-Musubi no Kami is identified as the fifth-generation ancestor of Ame-no-Oshihi, who in turn is the third-generation ancestor of Michinoomi no Mikoto. == History == [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, chouzusha.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Chōzuya}}]] The founding year of the shrine is unknown. According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an entry dated the 26th day of the 2nd month in the 9th year of Jōgan (867) mentions that the Hayashi Uji Shrine of Kawachi Province was recognized as a state shrine. Another entry from the 20th day of the 12th month in the 15th year of Jōgan (873) records the elevation of the enshrined deity Ame-no-Oshihi to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was burned down by the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the extinction of the Tomobayashi clan, local residents reconstructed the shrine as a place to worship their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine's identification as Tomobayashi Uji Shrine was debated among {{ill|Kokugaku|lt=Kokugaku scholars|ja|国学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Despite inquiries by Edo officials, local village leaders at the time claimed it was a shrine dedicated to Gozu Tennō. By the late Edo period, the shrine became recognized as a site for worshiping the ancestral deity of the Ōtomo clan, influenced by the work of {{ill|Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Kokugaku scholar from the village.Keita Endō, "Studies and Restoration of Shikinaisha: Investigation of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine" (First published in ''Shidai Nihonshi'', Vol. 5, 2002 / Collected in Endō, ''Formation of the Nihon Shoki and Related Materials'', Hanawa Shobō, 2015). In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. In 1932 (Shōwa 7), a survey commemorating the 50th anniversary of the {{ill|Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|ja|軍人勅諭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} confirmed that this shrine is the only one in Japan dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto, the military deity of the Ōtomo clan. Consequently, it gained attention as the "Western Yasukuni Shrine." This designation heavily relied on the interpretation of Kokugaku scholar and historian {{ill|Ikuta namekine|ja|生田目経徳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the Completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{translated page|ja|伴林氏神社|version=94918840|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]]

originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{translated page|ja|伴林氏神社|version=94918840|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tomobayashi Uji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera City|qq=}} Hayashi 3-chome 6-30 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 34 | Latitude seconds = 32.649 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 36 | Longitude seconds = 40.439 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Takami-Musubi no Kami|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi no Kami|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other names = | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = October 9 | Rituals = }} [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Tomobayashi Uji Shrine''' (伴林氏神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, in the Hayashi area of the former Haishi-go, Shiki District, Kawachi Province. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and previously held the Rank of {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}. It was once referred to as the "Western {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasukuni Shrine|qq=}}." == Deities == * Primary deity - {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} (たかむすひのかみ) * Secondary deities - {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (みちのおみのみこと), {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天押日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihi|qq=}} (あめのおしひのみこと) The shrine's name suggests that it was originally established by the {{ill|Tomobayashi clan|ja|伴林氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi clan|qq=}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo clan|qq=}}, to worship their ancestral deities. Takami-Musubi no Kami is identified as the fifth-generation ancestor of Ame-no-Oshihi, who in turn is the third-generation ancestor of Michinoomi no Mikoto. == History == [[File:Tomobayashiuji-jinja, chouzusha.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Chōzuya}}]] The founding year of the shrine is unknown. According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}, an entry dated the 26th day of the 2nd month in the 9th year of Jōgan (867) mentions that the Hayashi Uji Shrine of Kawachi Province was recognized as a state shrine. Another entry from the 20th day of the 12th month in the 15th year of Jōgan (873) records the elevation of the enshrined deity Ame-no-Oshihi to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|qq=}}, the shrine was burned down by the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}}. After the extinction of the Tomobayashi clan, local residents reconstructed the shrine as a place to worship their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine's identification as Tomobayashi Uji Shrine was debated among {{ill|Kokugaku|lt=Kokugaku scholars|ja|国学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugaku|qq=}}. Despite inquiries by Edo officials, local village leaders at the time claimed it was a shrine dedicated to Gozu Tennō. By the late Edo period, the shrine became recognized as a site for worshiping the ancestral deity of the Ōtomo clan, influenced by the work of {{ill|Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|ja|伴林光平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Mitsuhei|qq=}}, a Kokugaku scholar from the village.Keita Endō, "Studies and Restoration of Shikinaisha: Investigation of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine" (First published in ''Shidai Nihonshi'', Vol. 5, 2002 / Collected in Endō, ''Formation of the Nihon Shoki and Related Materials'', Hanawa Shobō, 2015). In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine. In 1932 (Shōwa 7), a survey commemorating the 50th anniversary of the {{ill|Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|ja|軍人勅諭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors|qq=}} confirmed that this shrine is the only one in Japan dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto, the military deity of the Ōtomo clan. Consequently, it gained attention as the "Western Yasukuni Shrine." This designation heavily relied on the interpretation of Kokugaku scholar and historian {{ill|Ikuta namekine|ja|生田目経徳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta namekine|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations and expansions led to the Completion tier 6]][[Category: of new shrine buildings in 1940 (Shōwa 15), including the relocation of a {{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=}} donated by {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasukuni Shrine|qq=}} in Tokyo. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in 1942 (Shōwa 17). The shrine marker was inscribed by {{ill|Fumimaro Konoe|ja|近衛文麿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumimaro Konoe|qq=}}. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Offering Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Worship Hall (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1940. * Corridor Fence (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Gate (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in 1940. * Shrine Office * Chōzuya (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Donated by Yasukuni Shrine in 1940; originally constructed in 1872. === Sub-Shrines === * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Registered Tangible Cultural Property) - Formerly the reliquary hall of Sawada Elementary School (now Dōmyōji Elementary School). == Cultural Properties == === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 100, June 29, 2022. * Worship Hall and Offering Hall of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Gate and Corridor Fence of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Chōzuya of Tomobayashi Uji Shrine - Registered June 29, 2022. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Hall (Former Sawada Elementary School Reliquary Hall) - Registered June 29, 2022. == location == * Address: Hayashi 3-chome 6-30, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture == Access == * By rail: 10-minute walk northwest (approximately 800m) from {{ill|Hajinonosato Station|ja|土師ノ里駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajinonosato Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Tomobayashiuji-jinja}} *[http://www.tomobayashiuji-jinja.jp/index.html Tomobayashi Uji Shrine] {{translated page|ja|伴林氏神社|version=94918840|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujidera]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Tomobayashi Shrine]] [[sn:omobayashi Shrine]] [[ja:伴林氏神社]] [[en:Tomobayashi Shrine]]
